qa_pairs.question: Why is the annual renewal of Digital Research Alliance of Canada accounts necessary, and what are the general consequences if an account is not renewed?
qa_pairs.answer: The annual renewal of Alliance accounts is essential for collecting up-to-date information required by granting agencies and for deactivating accounts that are no longer in use. If an account is not renewed between May 6 and June 9, 2026, it will be automatically deactivated, meaning the user will lose access to their data, although the data will not be immediately deleted. Deactivated accounts can still be renewed at any time.

qa_pairs.question: What is considered a primary role within the Digital Research Alliance of Canada system, and why is it important to keep it updated and renewed?
qa_pairs.answer: A primary role in the Alliance system corresponds to a user's main position, typically the one they will hold for the longest period (e.g., a full-time faculty appointment or a student's role with their primary supervisor). It is important because it assists in managing accounts with multiple roles, facilitates communication with users, and helps with reporting. Critically, if a primary role is not renewed during the annual renewal period, the associated Alliance account will be deactivated.

qa_pairs.question: What specific impact does a faculty member's (Principal Investigator's) failure to renew their Digital Research Alliance of Canada account have on their sponsored users?
qa_pairs.answer: If a faculty member (Principal Investigator) with sponsored users allows their Alliance account to expire without renewal by the deadline, their own account as well as the accounts of all users they sponsor will be deactivated. This will result in any jobs submitted by them or their sponsored users being paused, and their research group will lose access to their files until the sponsor's account is reactivated.
